purpose: Authoritative design document for ai-dev-envbuild; defines the layered tool-ownership model, global-vs-project-local dependency rules, folder structure, idempotency strategy, WSL filesystem guidance, GPU isolation policy, and GUI tool recommendations that all modules must follow.
invariants: Every tool must have exactly one install method and one layer — the no-duplication rule is the central invariant
Exactly two base PATH additions are canonical: ~/.local/bin (pipx) and ~/tools/bin (user scripts)
uv is the sole Python version manager — pyenv must not be introduced alongside it
Python libraries must not be installed globally — only CLIs (tools invoked by name) may be global via pipx
optional-heavy and optional-gpu groups must never run without an explicit flag
gotchas: Section §2 table shows five layers (system, runtimes, global Python CLI, project-local, containers) — new tools must fit one layer exactly; if a tool spans layers, it goes in the more isolated one
Section §4 explicitly states pytest, mypy, and other dev tools belong project-local (uv add --dev), not global — a global pytest cannot import project plugins
Section §8 WSL filesystem guidance: /mnt/c is for one-off reads only, not working trees — inotify watchers break on /mnt/c
Section §9 GPU: no Linux NVIDIA driver inside WSL — only the Windows host driver provides CUDA passthrough
Section §10 Ghidra is installed in WSL by default (Java app under WSLg); ImHex and Wireshark GUI are Windows-native preferred
